data.title: S&P 500 Rallies on Alphabet Jump. Nvidia Earnings Spark Market Optimism
data.description:
Broad market moves were muted on Wednesday as investors rotated into AI names ahead of Nvidia earnings.

The S&P 500 rose 0.2%, the Nasdaq Composite climbed 0.5% and the Dow slipped 0.3%, according to market data.

Alphabet led gains, jumping about 5% after rolling out its Gemini 3 model, lifting tech sentiment.

Alphabet's jump offset earlier losses across chip suppliers, which have seen heavy selling amid concern over AI capex sustainability this week.

Nvidia gained more than 2% in early trade as traders prepared for results after the close. Analysts expect strong data-center revenue, but the company faces a high bar on guidance.

Market watchers say Nvidia's outlook on hyperscaler spending will set near-term direction for AI-exposed stocks. Some strategists warn valuation pressure remains and cautious guidance could spark more selling in chips.

Retailers and other cyclicals showed mixed reactions to earnings, keeping the market tone fragile.

Investors will focus on Nvidia's report and management comments after the bell for clues on enterprise AI spending and year-end momentum.

data.title: Target Slips As Outlook Tightens And Sales Stay Weak
data.description:
Target slipped more than 2% in pre-market trading Wednesday after the retailer cut its full-year profit outlook and logged another quarter of sluggish demand. The company now expects FY25 earnings of $7$8 per share, narrowing the top end of its previous $7$9 range. Q3 earnings were a small bright spot, with adjusted EPS of $1.78 beating expectations of $1.71.

Revenue came in at $25.3 billion, matching estimates but down 1.5% from last year. Comparable sales fell 2.7%, a deeper drop than analysts anticipated, as shoppers continued to pull back on non-essential categories like apparel and home decor. Incoming CEO Michael Fiddelke, who steps in next February, said the quarter was volatile, pointing to pressure from the SNAP funding pause and the government shutdown.

Target expects similar softness in Q4 and is guiding for a low single-digit sales decline. A fuller outlook will arrive after the holidays. The company also plans to add another $1 billion to next year's investment budget, bringing total new spending to $5 billion aimed at improving stores, merchandise and digital systems.

data.title: US canceling October's employment report after shutdown prevented data collection
data.description:
The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics said on Wednesday it would not be publishing the closely watched employment report for October, but will combine nonfarm payrolls for that month with November's report after the recently ended government shutdown prevented the collection of data for the household survey.

That means October's unemployment rate will never be known, something that the White House had warned would happen.

"Establishment survey data from the Current Employment Statistics survey for October 2025 will be published with the November 2025 data," BLS said in a statement. "Household survey data from the Current Population Survey could not be collected for the October 2025 reference period due to a lapse in appropriations. The household survey data is not able to be retroactively collected."

The establishment survey covers the nonfarm payrolls component of the employment report. The unemployment rate and other ratios are calculated from the household survey.

The BLS will on Thursday publish September's employment report, which was delayed by the 43-day government shutdown.

November's employment report will be released on December 16, the BLS said. That would be after the Federal Reserve's December 9-10 policy meeting.

"The collection period for November 2025 data will be extended for both surveys, and extra processing time will be added," the agency said.

data.title: Cash App to Launch Buy Now, Pay Later Debit Card
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Block's payment platform Cash App is launching a new debit card that will allow holders to pay in installments at any location where Visa is accepted.

Cash App said Wednesday it plans to debut the Visa Debit Flex Card, which will give users the option to pay with Block's buy now, pay later feature Afterpay. Using Afterpay, customers can take out a loan to buy something and pay it off incrementally over the course of six weeks.

Afterpay purchases can be made anywhere online or in-store where Visa is accepted, the company said. Customers can order a Visa Debit Flex Card and turn Afterpay on and off through the Cash App app.

Cash App's news comes as buy now, pay later companies such as Affirm and Klarna are pushing their debit card offerings. Offering a card is designed to make it easier for customers to use buy now, pay later services with more vendors and in more situations. The loans have become more popular among consumers in recent years as many of them come with 0% interest, unlike traditional credit-card debt.

Cash App will start the program as a pilot in the coming months, with plans for a larger rollout in early 2026, it said.

Instead of looking backward at credit scores, Cash App's platform determines eligibility for a buy now, pay later loan by evaluating customers' current financial behaviors, such as cash-flow patterns, spending habits, savings and payment consistency.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Musk's xAI and Nvidia to Develop Data Center in Saudi Arabia — WSJ
data.description:
By Lauren Thomas and Amrith Ramkumar

Elon Musk's artificial-intelligence company xAI will work with chip maker Nvidia and a Saudi Arabian partner to develop a giant data center in the kingdom, Musk said Wednesday at an event coinciding with Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman's visit to the U.S.

Musk and Nvidia Chief Executive Jensen Huang said they were teaming up with Saudi Arabia's artificial-intelligence company, Humain, on a data center that will consume 500 megawatts, or enough electricity to power several hundred thousand homes for a year.

The announcement came at the U.S.-Saudi Investment Forum, an event at the Kennedy Center featuring CEOs and government officials. President Trump is expected to speak at the summit and tout Saudi Arabia's investments in the U.S., as well as the AI partnership between the two countries.

Musk's xAI is one of several AI companies expanding in the Middle East, which offers copious land and energy as well as sovereign-wealth funds eager to finance development. Nvidia, Advanced Micro Devices and Qualcomm announced billions of dollars in investment in Saudi Arabia in May, when Trump visited the kingdom. Many companies are also striking deals in the United Arab Emirates.

The U.S. is expected to soon approve the export of advanced AI chips to Humain and its U.S. partners, moving ahead with work that began earlier in the year, people familiar with the discussions said. The agreement would include security guardrails to ensure the chips wouldn't benefit China, some of the people said.

xAI, OpenAI and other U.S. companies need government approval to export chips to the region because of those security concerns. They are competing against one another to build the largest data centers capable of training the most advanced models.

Wednesday's event highlighted a recent shift among businesses to embrace Saudi Arabia after many cut ties with the kingdom following the 2018 murder of Washington Post journalist Jamal Khashoggi. Trump on Tuesday said the crown prince "knew nothing" about the murder, contradicting a CIA assessment.

Other companies investing in data centers in Saudi Arabia or joining with Humain include private-equity firm Blackstone and Cisco Systems, their executives said at the event.

Blackstone's Steve Schwarzman said his firm will start building AI data warehouses in Saudi Arabia.

Write to Lauren Thomas at lauren.thomas@wsj.com and Amrith Ramkumar at amrith.ramkumar@wsj.com

data.title: Brazil judge flags BRB role in Banco Master fraud; losses may top $1.9 bln
data.description:
By Marcela Ayres

A Brazilian judge said there is evidence that executives at state-run lender BRB knowingly joined an alleged fraud at Banco Master, a scheme that could saddle BRB with losses exceeding 10 billion reais ($1.87 billion).

The ruling by federal Judge Ricardo Augusto Leite, seen by Reuters, authorized the arrest of Master's controlling shareholder and the temporary removal of BRB's top executives.

It sheds light on BRB's involvement in Master's collapse, which prompted the central bank to liquidate the privately held lender on Tuesday over severe liquidity problems and regulatory breaches.

"There is emerging evidence that BRB executives knowingly took part in the alleged fraudulent scheme devised by Banco Master's managers," Leite wrote, citing signs of potential fraudulent management at BRB.

Banco Master and BRB did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

Controlled by the regional Federal District government, BRB said earlier on Wednesday that its board decided to hire a specialized external audit firm to investigate the facts related to the police operation on Tuesday.

At the center of the probe is BRB's purchase of credit securities from Master, some tied to non-existent assets, to ease Master's liquidity crunch. Transfers between the lenders totaled 16.7 billion reais from July 2024 to October 2025, according to the ruling.

Leite said that BRB's purchase of credit portfolios from Master made up 30% of its assets, suggesting the public bank sought to prop up Master during its liquidity crisis.

The actions point to "a common predisposition and means for carrying out an indeterminate series of crimes" by Master executives, later joined by BRB's leadership, Leite said in the document.

"This acquiescence by the public institution was the most serious so far and could result in losses exceeding 10 billion reais," he said.

In March, BRB announced plans to acquire Master - a deal later blocked by regulators - while executives arranged 12.2 billion reais in transfers from BRB between January and May to keep Master afloat, Leite said.

That amount matched Master's shortfall after it issued roughly 50 billion reais in debt while holding a portfolio made up largely of low-liquidity assets. Leite ordered the amount frozen from the private assets of a series of executives and companies involved in the case, including BRB and Master.

To plug the gap, Master allegedly acquired loan portfolios from Tirreno Consultoria Promotoria de Crédito e Participações S.A. without making any payment, investigators said, describing the firm as a shell company for payroll-deductible loans that did not exist, but that were originated by financial institution Cartos.

Cartos said it is not a target of the investigation and that no wrongdoing has been attributed to the company, which it said follows strict compliance standards. Reuters was unable to reach Tirreno for comment.

Police on Tuesday arrested Banco Master's owner Daniel Vorcaro, among other executives, and raided BRB's headquarters as well as the homes of its then-CEO Paulo Henrique Costa and CFO Dario Oswaldo Garcia Junior.

($1 = 5.3356 reais)

data.title: Xiaomi's EV Unit Turns First Profit As Sales Surge
data.description:
Xiaomi's (XIACF) electric-vehicle division just hit a big milestone, reporting its first-ever profit in Q3. The EV unit posted an operating profit of 700 million yuan in the September quarter a major turnaround from the 300 million-yuan loss it logged just a quarter earlier. For a business that only launched its first car last year, it's a pretty striking jump.

A lot of that momentum comes down to demand. Xiaomi sold nearly 109,000 EVs during the quarter and is still aiming for 350,000 deliveries this year. It's an aggressive target, but it shows how quickly the company is scaling in a market where competition is famously cutthroat. This early progress is also fueling Xiaomi's confidence as it works toward entering the European market by 2027.

At the same time, leadership is trying to keep expectations grounded. President Lu Weibing said margins may soften next year as China trims tax incentives and rival models flood the market. Xiaomi is proving it can find its footing and real profitability in one of the toughest EV arenas in the world.

data.title: THEON Invited by OCCAR to Sign Contract Revision
data.description:
PRESS RELEASE

Bloomberg (THEON:NA) / Reuters (THEON.AS)

19 November 2025 - Theon Sensors SA, fully owned subsidiary of Theon International Plc (THEON) is pleased to announce that it has been officially invited by the Organisation for Joint Armament Cooperation (OCCAR), together with Hensoldt AG (HENSOLDT), to sign the first Contract Revision of its existing contract for the supply of additional advanced man-portable equipment.

Pursuant to last week's formal approval procedures by the two participating nations, Germany and Belgium, THEON and HENSOLDT will proceed with the official signing of the OCCAR Contract Revision on December 10, 2025, for the purchase of several thousand systems which are expected to materially increase THEON's existing backlog. As per customary procedures, details of this Contract Revision will be announced at market closing of same date.

For inquiries, please contact:

 
Investor Relations     Media Contact 
 Nikos Malesiotis       Elli Michou 
 E-Mail: ir@theon.com   E-Mail: press@theon.com 
 Tel: +30 210 6772290   Tel: +30 210 6728610

About THEON GROUP

THEON GROUP of companies develops and manufactures cutting-edge night vision and thermal Imaging systems for Defense and Security applications with a global footprint. THEON GROUP started its operations in 1997 from Greece and today occupies a leading role in the sector thanks to its international presence through subsidiaries and production facilities in Greece, Cyprus, Germany, the Baltics, the United States, the Gulf States, Switzerland, Denmark, Belgium, Singapore and South Korea. THEON GROUP has more than 240,000 systems in service with Armed and Special Forces in 71 countries around the world, 26 of which are NATO countries. RNATIONAL PLC has been listed on Euronext Amsterdam (AMS: THEON) since February 2024.

www.theon.com

Attachment

  • Press Release Trading Update- OCCAR revision_19_11_2025_vF

data.title: Correction: Fitch Affirms 2 Genova Hipotecario Spanish RMBS Transactions
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-Madrid-19 November 2025:

This is a correction of a release issued on 17 November 2025, to include the relevant ESG Scores for both transactions.

Fitch Ratings has affirmed AyT Genova Hipotecario XI, FTH and AyT Genova Hipotecario XII, FTH. Fitch has revised the Outlook on Genova XI's class C and D notes and Genova XII's class B notes to Positive from Stable. A full list of rating actions is below.

AyT Genova Hipotecario XI, FTH

----Class A2 ES0312302013; Long Term Rating; Affirmed; A+sf; Rating Outlook Stable

----Class B ES0312302021; Long Term Rating; Affirmed; A+sf; Rating Outlook Stable

----Class C ES0312302039; Long Term Rating; Affirmed; Asf; Rating Outlook Positive

----Class D ES0312302047; Long Term Rating; Affirmed; Asf; Rating Outlook Positive

AyT Genova Hipotecario XII, FTH

----Class A ES0312285002; Long Term Rating; Affirmed; A+sf; Rating Outlook Stable

----Class B ES0312285010; Long Term Rating; Affirmed; Asf; Rating Outlook Positive

Transaction Summary

The RMBS transactions are Spanish residential mortgage securitisations serviced by CaixaBank, S.A. (A-/Positive/F1).

KEY RATING DRIVERS

Ratings Capped by Counterparty Risks: The transactions' maximum achievable rating remains 'A+sf' due to the transaction account bank (TAB) minimum eligibility ratings being set at 'BBB+' or 'F2', which are not compatible with 'AAsf' or 'AAAsf' rating categories, as per Fitch's Counterparty Criteria.

The maximum achievable rating for Genova XI's class C and D notes and Genova XII's class B notes is equivalent to the respective TAB deposit rating (CaixaBank S.A., A-/Positive/F1, deposit rating 'A'). This is because the cash reserves held at the TAB represent a material source of credit enhancement (CE) for the notes, and the sudden loss of these funds would imply a model-implied downgrade of 10 or more notches in accordance with Fitch's criteria. The revision of the Outlook to Positive on these notes mirrors the recent rating action on the TAB.

Stable Asset Performance Expectation: The rating actions reflect the transactions' broadly stable asset performance outlook, in line with our neutral asset performance outlook for eurozone RMBS. The transactions have a low share of loans in arrears over 90 days (less than 0.4% of the outstanding pool balance as of the latest reporting dates excluding defaults), and gross cumulative defaults (defined as loans in arrears over 18 months) are around 2.0% relative to the initial pool balance.

Both portfolios have ample seasoning of around 19 years, and low current LTV ratios below 25%. For both deals, the portfolio credit analysis remains driven by the minimum loss vector (e.g. 5% at the 'AAA' rating case).

Sufficient CE: The rating actions also reflect Fitch's view that CE for the notes is sufficient to fully compensate the credit and cash flow stresses associated with the ratings. We expect structural CE ratios to continue increasing for Genova XI, driven by amortisation of the notes with the reserve fund close to the absolute floor. However, we expect CE volatility for Genova XII if the reserve fund is permitted to amortises to its absolute floor, subject to performance triggers.

We forecast that CE will increase more rapidly for both transactions once their portfolio factors reach 10% of the initial pool balance and activates mandatory sequential amortisation of the notes, versus 13% and 14% for Genova XI and XII, respectively, as of the latest reporting dates. Both transactions' excess spread levels remain limited and in line with historical averages, and we expect this to continue, driven by the low yield payable by the assets (the weighted average margin on the portfolios is approximately 0.4% a year).

Genova XI and Genova XII have high ESG scores for Transaction Parties & Operational Risk. The TAB eligibility triggers have been changed during the life of the transactions with a material impact on the ratings. The initially defined eligibility ratings of 'A' and 'F1' as of closing were modified to 'BBB+' or 'F2' in March 2016.

RATING SENSITIVITIES Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

For Genova XI's class C and D notes and Genova XII's class B notes, a downgrade of the TAB provider's deposit rating, as the notes are rated at their maximum achievable rating due to excessive counterparty risk exposure.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

For Genova XI's class A2 and B notes and Genova XII's class A notes, a change in the TAB minimum eligibility rating thresholds compatible with 'AAsf' or 'AAAsf' ratings, as per Fitch's Structured Finance and Covered Bonds Counterparty Rating Criteria.

For Genova XI's class C and D notes and Genova XII's class B notes, an upgrade of the TAB provider's deposit rating, as the notes are rated at their maximum achievable rating due to excessive counterparty risk exposure.

USE OF THIRD PARTY DUE DILIGENCE PURSUANT TO SEC RULE 17G -10

Form ABS Due Diligence-15E was not provided to, or reviewed by, Fitch in relation to this rating action.

DATA ADEQUACY

AyT Genova Hipotecario XI, FTH, AyT Genova Hipotecario XII, FTH

Fitch has checked the consistency and plausibility of the information it has received about the performance of the asset pools and the transactions. Fitch has not reviewed the results of any third-party assessment of the asset portfolio information or conducted a review of origination files as part of its ongoing monitoring.

Fitch did not undertake a review of the information provided about the underlying asset pools ahead of the transactions' ] initial closing. The subsequent performance of the transactions over the years is consistent with the agency's expectations given the operating environment and Fitch is therefore satisfied that the asset pool information relied upon for its initial rating analysis was adequately reliable.

Overall, and together with any assumptions referred to above, Fitch's assessment of the information relied upon for the agency's rating analysis according to its applicable rating methodologies indicates that it is adequately reliable.

REFERENCES FOR SUBSTANTIALLY MATERIAL SOURCE CITED AS KEY DRIVER OF RATING The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria. PUBLIC RATINGS WITH CREDIT LINKAGE TO OTHER RATINGS

Genova XI's class C and D notes and Genova XII's class B notes' ratings are capped and linked at the transaction account bank's deposit rating due to excessive counterparty exposure.

ESG Considerations

Genova XI and Genova XII have ESG Relevance Scores of '5' for "Transaction Parties & Operational Risk" due to modification of TAB replacement triggers after transaction closing and excessive reliance on transaction counterparties, which has a negative impact on the credit profile, and is highly relevant to the rating, resulting in a change to the rating of at least a one-notch downgrade

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit-neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores are not inputs in the rating process; they are an observation on the relevance and materiality of ESG factors in the rating decision. For more information on Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores, visit https://www.fitchratings.com/topics/esg/products#esg-relevance-scores.

data.title: Thessaloniki Port Authority Signs EUR 195.6 Million Pier 6 Expansion Contract With METKA–TEKAL JV
data.description:
Thessaloniki Port Authority SA :

  • SIGNS EUR 195.6 MILLION CONTRACT WITH JV OF METKA–TEKAL FOR THE PIER 6 INFRASTRUCTURE EXPANSION PROJECT

  • PIER 6 EXPANSION TO ADD 513 METRES LENGTH, DEEPEN CHANNEL FOR ULCV VESSEL

  • DEADLINE FOR THE COMPLETION OF THE PROJECT IS SET AT 40 MONTHS

  • TO FUND PIER 6 PROJECT VIA OWN CAPITAL, LOANS

data.title: BofA clients continue to log out of tech stocks
data.description:
Key points:
  • US indexes modestly higher, Nasdaq up 0.3%
  • Comm svcs leads S&P 500 sector gains; Energy weakest group
  • Dollar rallies ~0.5%; gold edges up; crude down >2%; bitcoin down >3%
  • US 10-Year Treasury yield edges down to ~4.11%

BOFA CLIENTS CONTINUE TO LOG OUT OF TECH STOCKS

BofA Securities equity and quant strategist Jill Carey Hall says that last week, with the S&P 500 index gaining just 0.1%, clients sold U.S. equities, led by large outflows from single stocks ($4.4 billion) for a third week in a row. Clients bought equity ETFs for a fifth straight week.

Clients offloaded stocks across size segments, but flows into large-cap ETFs led clients to be net buyers of large cap equities overall.

"Institutional clients led the selling after buying the dip the prior week; retail clients were also sellers (for the 2nd straight week after buying most weeks since mid-Sept.). Hedge funds were the sole net buyers after selling the prior week," writes Hall in her note.

She adds that corporate client buybacks ramped up for a second week but have remained below typical seasonal levels throughout this earnings season. Tech buybacks (rolling 12-week sum) have slowed to a $7-8 billion run rate this month vs a $9-10 billion run rate since August.

Hall says that Tech stocks saw the biggest outflows. In fact, the group has now seen outflows for five straight weeks. Staples and Real Estate saw the biggest outflows after Tech.

Only Healthcare and Energy saw inflows, soley from institutional clients.

In terms of ETFs, Hall says clients bought equity ETFs for a fifth straight week, with inflows across major styles (Blend/Growth/Value) and sizes (Small/Mid/Large/Broad Market). That said, style flows continue to skew toward Value over Growth.

Clients bought ETFs in eight of the 11 sectors, led by Materials and Healthcare; Tech ETFs also saw inflows despite single-stock outflows.

Financials ETFs saw the largest outflows.
